releases.description: This release introduced the island, the survivors, and the first hints of the monster.

notes.title: Oceanic Flight 815 crashes on the island
notes.note_md:
Flight from Sydney to Los Angeles breaks apart mid-air and crashes on a mysterious island in the South Pacific.

- **Survivors** discover they are not alone
- The cockpit section lands in the jungle
- A **smoke monster** is heard in the distance

![Flight 815 wreckage](https://via.placeholder.com/480x270/1a237e/fff?text=Oceanic+815+Wreckage)

notes.title: Jack, Kate and Charlie form the first group
notes.note_md:
Dr Jack Shephard tends to the wounded. Kate is revealed to be a fugitive. Charlie is the bassist from Drive Shaft.

### Key moments
1. *"Live together, die alone"* — later becomes a motto
2. The **transceiver** picks up a French broadcast (16 years old)
3. Locke shows Walt the backgammon board: *"Two players. Two sides. One is light, one is dark."*

![Survivors on the beach](https://via.placeholder.com/480x270/004d40/fff?text=Beach+Camp)

notes.title: The monster attacks the pilot
notes.note_md:
When Jack, Kate and Charlie go into the jungle to find the cockpit, they encounter something in the trees. The **pilot** is killed.

Nobody knows what the monster is. It sounds mechanical and organic at once. 🌲👁️

notes.title: The Hatch and the Dharma Initiative
notes.note_md:
Locke and Boone find a metal hatch in the ground. Inside: a ladder, a computer, and the **Swan station** — part of the Dharma Initiative.

> Every 108 minutes someone must enter the numbers (4, 8, 15, 16, 23, 42) and push the button.

![The Hatch](https://via.placeholder.com/480x270/311b92/fff?text=The+Hatch)

notes.title: The Others are revealed
notes.note_md:
The **Others** are not primitive island dwellers. They have homes, rules, and a leader (Ben Linus).

- They take Walt from Michael
- They have a list of "good people"
- They live in **Barracks** with modern amenities

![Dharma logo](https://via.placeholder.com/400x225/ff6f00/fff?text=DHARMA+Initiative)

notes.title: Desmond and the fail-safe
notes.note_md:
**Desmond Hume** has been in the Swan for years. If the button is not pushed in time, he believes something very bad will happen.

When the timer reaches zero, he turns the fail-safe key. The sky turns **purple** and the electromagnetic event discharges. ⚡

notes.title: We have to go back — the flashforward
notes.note_md:
The season finale reveals that **Jack and Kate get off the island**. But Jack is broken and addicted; he wants to go *back*.

*"We have to go back, Kate. We were not supposed to leave."*

![Jack and Kate off the island](https://via.placeholder.com/480x270/4a148c/fff?text=We+Have+To+Go+Back)

notes.title: Jacob and the Man in Black
notes.note_md:
The island has a **protector** (Jacob) and an enemy (the Man in Black / Smoke Monster).

- Jacob brings people to the island as candidates
- The Man in Black wants to leave and destroy the island
- **Richard Alpert** has been there for centuries

🏝️⚔️

notes.title: The Sideways and the church
notes.note_md:
### The end

In the **sideways** (afterlife), the survivors find each other again. In a church, Jack's father says:

> *"There is no now here."* — This is a place you all made together so you could find one another.

They move on together. 💫

notes.title: The island is left in Hurley's care
notes.note_md:
Jack passes the role of **protector** to Hurley. Ben stays as his advisor.

*"I've had a lot of experience with the island. I could help."* — Ben

![Final scene](https://via.placeholder.com/480x270/1b5e20/fff?text=The+End)

notes.title: Unanswered mysteries (pending)
notes.note_md:
Some questions remain:

- What exactly was the **smoke monster**?
- Why were the numbers cursed?
- Was it all **real** or partly in the afterlife?

*"I'll see you in another life, brotha."* — Desmond

🔮
